blocks.body: Future Perfect Continuous: Time Expressions — for, by, by the time, by then, how long, all day, all morning, since.

{"level":"B2","intro":"Expressions like for, by, by the time, by then, how long, all day, all morning, since often go with Future Perfect Continuous because they show duration or a process up to a future limit.","rules":[{"label":"for + period","color":"emerald","text":"For shows how long the process will already have been continuing by a certain moment.","example":"By noon, I will have been studying for two hours."},{"label":"by / by the time / by then","color":"blue","text":"These expressions set the future limit by which the duration will already have built up.","example":"By next month, she will have been working here for a year. / By the time you arrive, we will have been driving for six hours."},{"label":"how long / all day / all morning","color":"amber","text":"These words help highlight duration and process before a future point.","example":"How long will they have been waiting by then? / He will have been travelling all day."},{"label":"since with future reference","color":"violet","text":"Since sounds natural when a future point looks back at a clear starting point of the process.","example":"By next June, she will have been living here since 2020."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common markers for this section.","items":[{"label":"Duration","title":"for two hours / for ten years","subtitle":"We show how long the process will already have lasted by a future limit."},{"label":"Future limit","title":"by next month / by then / by noon","subtitle":"We give the moment by which the process will already have been continuing for some time."},{"label":"Future clause","title":"by the time you arrive / by the time the meeting starts","subtitle":"We connect the duration with another future event."},{"label":"Starting point","title":"since 2020 / since January / since breakfast","subtitle":"The starting point sounds natural when we look back from a future moment."}]}

{"title":"2. How these expressions work","sections":[{"label":"for + period","color":"emerald","description":"For gives a duration: two hours, ten years, several months, and so on.","examples":[{"en":"By 5 p.m., I will have been working for two hours.","ua":"By five o'clock, the process will already have lasted two hours."},{"en":"By next June, they will have been living here for ten years.","ua":"By next June, the duration will already be ten years."}]},{"label":"by / by the time / by then","color":"blue","description":"These expressions give the future limit or the other event up to which the process will still be continuing.","examples":[{"en":"By next month, she will have been studying for six months.","ua":"By sets the future limit."},{"en":"By the time you arrive, we will have been waiting for an hour.","ua":"By the time connects the process with another future event."}]},{"label":"how long / all day / all morning","color":"amber","description":"These words help you state or ask directly about the duration of the process.","examples":[{"en":"How long will you have been working on it by Friday?","ua":"We ask about accumulated duration by Friday."},{"en":"By dinner, he will have been travelling all day.","ua":"All day stresses a long continuous process."}]},{"label":"since + starting point","color":"violet","description":"Since works naturally when a future point looks back at a known starting point of the process.","examples":[{"en":"By then, I will have been living in Warsaw since 2024.","ua":"The future moment looks back at a clear start."},{"en":"By next spring, she will have been working remotely since January.","ua":"Since works well with a specific starting point."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common combinations","intro":"See how time expressions connect with Future Perfect Continuous.","rows":[{"en":"By noon, I will have been studying for two hours.","ua":"for two hours / by noon","note":"duration up to a future hour"},{"en":"By next month, she will have been working here for a year.","ua":"by next month","note":"a future limit"},{"en":"By the time you arrive, we will have been driving for six hours.","ua":"by the time","note":"a process up to another future event"},{"en":"How long will they have been waiting by then?","ua":"how long / by then","note":"a question about duration up to a future point"},{"en":"By next June, he will have been living there since 2020.","ua":"since 2020","note":"a future look back at the start of the process"}],"warning":"Do not mix up for and since. After by the time in a future time clause, we usually do not use will."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using by instead of for for duration.","wrong":"❌ By noon, I will have been studying by two hours.","right":"✅ By noon, I will have been studying for two hours."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using will after by the time.","wrong":"❌ By the time you will arrive, we will have been waiting for an hour.","right":"✅ By the time you arrive, we will have been waiting for an hour."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing up since and for.","wrong":"❌ By next June, she will have been living here since ten years.","right":"✅ By next June, she will have been living here for ten years."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["for gives duration: for two hours, for ten years.","by, by then, and by the time give the future limit or event.","How long, all day, and all morning highlight process and duration.","since sounds natural when a future point looks back at a specific starting point.","After by the time in a future time clause, we usually do not use will."]}

{"title":"Future Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":true}]}
